text: The distance between points \( (x_1,y_1,z_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2,z_2) \) in 3D is:
name: 3D Distance Formula

text: The direction cosines \( l, m, n \) of a line satisfy:
name: Direction Cosines Identity

text: The equation of a plane in normal form is:
explanation: Normal form of plane: \( lx+my+nz=p \), where \(l,m,n\) are direction cosines of normal and \(p>0\).
name: Plane Normal Form

text: The angle \(\theta\) between lines with direction cosines \( (l_1,m_1,n_1) \) and \( (l_2,m_2,n_2) \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = l_1l_2+m_1m_2+n_1n_2 \). Lines are perpendicular when this equals 0.
name: Angle Between Lines in 3D

text: The intercept form of a plane is:
explanation: Intercept form: \( \frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{z}{c}=1 \), where \(a,b,c\) are x,y,z intercepts.
name: Plane Intercept Form

text: The symmetric form of a line through \((x_1,y_1,z_1)\) with direction ratios \((a,b,c)\) is:
name: Symmetric Form of Line

text: The distance from point \( (x_1,y_1,z_1) \) to plane \( ax+by+cz+d=0 \) is:
explanation: Distance from point to plane: \( d = \frac{|ax_1+by_1+cz_1+d|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2+c^2}} \)
name: Point to Plane Distance

text: Two planes are perpendicular if their normals \( (a_1,b_1,c_1) \) and \( (a_2,b_2,c_2) \) satisfy:
explanation: Planes are perpendicular when their normals are perpendicular: \( a_1a_2+b_1b_2+c_1c_2=0 \)
name: Perpendicular Planes

text: The centroid of tetrahedron with vertices \( A,B,C,D \) is:
explanation: Centroid of tetrahedron = average of all 4 vertices' coordinates.
name: Centroid of Tetrahedron

text: Direction cosines of the x-axis are:
explanation: The x-axis has direction ratios \((1,0,0)\), which are also direction cosines since magnitude = 1.
name: Direction Cosines of Axes

text: The angle between two planes is equal to the angle between their:
explanation: Dihedral angle between planes = angle between their normal vectors.
name: Angle Between Planes

text: The equation of plane through three points can be found using:
name: Plane Through 3 Points

text: The shortest distance between two skew lines \( \vec{r}=\vec{a_1}+\lambda\vec{b_1} \) and \( \vec{r}=\vec{a_2}+\mu\vec{b_2} \) is:
explanation: Shortest distance between skew lines: \( SD = \frac{|(\vec{a_2}-\vec{a_1})\cdot(\vec{b_1}\times\vec{b_2})|}{|\vec{b_1}\times\vec{b_2}|} \)
name: Shortest Distance - Skew Lines

text: The coordinates of a point dividing segment \(AB\) (\(A=(x_1,y_1,z_1)\), \(B=(x_2,y_2,z_2)\)) in ratio \(m:n\) internally are:
name: 3D Section Formula

text: The sphere with center \((a,b,c)\) and radius \(r\) has equation:
name: Equation of Sphere
